original text:
And remove the [feature]Enable internal plugin white list.
But all of them can't solve my problem.
Finally I synthesize the methods above and solve the problem.
Because I come across the issue when I am in Mozilla build bash, I try to rebase the file msys-1.0.dll ( not windows cmd ), use admin rights to run Mozilla build bash, and reboot my OS.
After some days' test, the problem disappeared!
I take some time to read Firefox build on Android and try to put it into practice, but at present I have no plan to release an available Firefox build edition on Android.
They are not compatible with -mfpu=neon, which has been fixed in gcc side ( https://gcc.gnu.org/ml/gcc-patches/2016-01/msg00248.html ). Considering some LTO bugs, we may rebuild gcc and binutils ourselves in order to use LTO ( avoiding some ICE ).
-mfpu=neon exists in https://dxr.mozilla.org/mozilla-central/search?q=mfpu%3Dneon&redirect=false&case=true, if we don't patch gcc and binutils, we can add -fno-lto to the Makefile.
And firefox's stlport also can't use LTO, which will not find some symbols, but I have no time to check it.
Some modules will cause symbols error.
At present I don't know how to make it work on Android.
processed_text:
And remove the [feature]Enable internal plugin white list.
But all of them can't solve my problem.
Finally I synthesize the methods above and solve the problem.
Because I come across the issue when I am in Mozilla build bash, I try to rebase the file msys-1.0.dll ( not windows cmd ), use admin rights to run Mozilla build bash, and reboot my OS.
After some days' test, the problem disappeared!
I take some time to read Firefox build on Android and try to put it into practice, but at present I have no plan to release an available Firefox build edition on Android.
They are not compatible with -mfpu=neon, which has been fixed in gcc side (  ). Considering some LTO bugs, we may rebuild gcc and binutils ourselves in order to use LTO ( avoiding some ICE ).
-mfpu=neon exists in , if we don't patch gcc and binutils, we can add -fno-lto to the Makefile.
And firefox's stlport also can't use LTO, which will not find some symbols, but I have no time to check it.
Some modules will cause symbols error.
At present I don't know how to make it work on Android.

original text:
WISCONSIN RAPIDS - Music, children's shows, theater, poetry and film screenings are just a few of the nearly 50 events that are part of an arts festival in March.
The fourth annual ARTi Gras festival will kick off March 9 and run through March 18 in Wisconsin Rapids, Nekoosa and Port Edwards. This year, the festival will also include events in Marshfield, Stevens Point, Amherst, Bancroft and Almond.
Take a look at our guide of events happening throughout central Wisconsin for ARTi Gras 2017.
Champagne Gala Opening: "An Evening with Shakespeare" at 7 p.m. at the Wisconsin Rapids Community Theatre auditorium, 220 Third Ave. S. in Wisconsin Rapids. This event is $20.
Children's musical, "Little Pea" at 3 p.m. at Alexander Middle School, 540 Birch St. in Nekoosa. Artwork was created by Alexander Middle School students. This event is free.
Children's musical, "Little Pea" at 5 p.m. at the Boys & Girls Club of the Wisconsin Rapids Area, 501 17th St. S. in Wisconsin Rapids. Artwork was created by the Boys & Girls Club members. This event is free.
Cranberry Artwork 2016 Opening Reception at 5:30 p.m. at the Central Wisconsin Cultural Center, 240 Johnson St. in Wisconsin Rapids. This event is free.
Blue Monday Band performs at 7 p.m. at Chestnut Center for the Arts, 208 S. Chestnut Ave. in Marshfield. This event is $10.
"Seussical the Musical" will be performed at 7 p.m. at the Nekoosa High School, 500 S. Cedar St. in Nekoosa. The event is $10.
"Venus in Fur" by David Ives, an adult play performed by the Prague Shakespeare Company starts at 7:30 p.m. at the WRCT auditorium. This event is $20.
Children's musical, "Little Pea" at 9 a.m. at the Portage County Library, 1001 Main St. in Stevens Point. Artwork was created by the UWSP Art Department. This event is free.
Children's musical, "Little Pea" at 11:30 a.m. at McMillan Memorial Library, 490 E. Grand Ave. in Wisconsin Rapids. Artwork was created by the Grand Avenue Artists. This event is free.
Children's musical, "Little Pea" at 1:30 p.m. at John Edwards High School, 801 Second St. in Port Edwards. Artwork was created by Port Edwards students. This event is free.
"Seussical the Musical" will be performed at 2 p.m. at the Nekoosa High School, 500 S. Cedar St. in Nekoosa. The event is $10.
Children's musical, "Little Pea" at 3:30 p.m. at Bancroft Elementary School, 5590 School Road in Bancroft. Artwork was created by Bancroft students. This event is free.
"Seussical the Musical" will be performed at 7 p.m. at the Nekoosa High School. The event is $10.
"Love Letters" by A.R. Gurney at 7 p.m. at Chestnut Center for the Arts in Marshfield. This event is $10.
Poetry slam with former Wisconsin Poet Laureate, Bruce Dethlefsen at 12 p.m. at the Alexander House, Center for Art and History, 1131 Wisconsin River Drive in Port Edwards. This event is free.
Silver Foxes present "Nagging, American Style" at 2 p.m. at the WRCT auditorium. This event is free.
Shakespeare Workshop led by the Prague Shakespeare Company for ages 16 years old and older from 3 p.m. to 6 p.m. at the WRCT auditorium. This event is $15, and advance registration is strongly recommended.
WRCT Improv Night at 6 p.m. at the WRCT auditorium. This event is $5.
Oscar-nominated foreign documentary film showing: a family confronts their brother's killers during genocide in Indonesia at 1:30 p.m. and 7 p.m. at McMillan Memorial Library. This event is free.
Pianist Pete Revelle performs at 1:30 p.m. at the Retirement Community of Nekoosa.
Silver Foxes present "Nagging, American Style" at 2 p.m. at the Lettie W. Jensen Community Center, 487 n. Main St. in Amherst. This event is free.
A foreign documentary film showing: four women decide whether to wear a hijab at 4 p.m. at the McMillan Memorial Library. This event is free.
Blue Monday Band performs at 7 p.m. at Hollyrocks, 114 Second St. N. in Wisconsin Rapids. This event is free.
Pete Revelle & Friends will perform at 1 p.m. at the Central Wisconsin Cultural Center. This event is free.
Port Edwards Public Schools Youth Art exhibit from 1 p.m. to 4 p.m. at the Alexander House. This event is free.
Students from the University of Wisconsin - Stevens Point theater program present a Musical Theatre Night and Reception at 7 p.m. at the WRCT auditorium. This event is $10.
Blue Monday Band performs at 1:30 p.m. at the Retirement Community of Nekoosa. This event is free.
WRCT performs "The Gin Game" by D.L. Coburn at 7 p.m. at Almond-Bancroft High School, 1336 Elm St. in Almond. This event is free.
Amanda Meidl stars in "Shirley Valentine" by Willy Russell at 7 p.m. at the WRCT auditorium. This event is $15.
The Central Wisconsin Choristers perform a spring concert at 4:30 p.m. at the Central Wisconsin Cultural Center. This event is free.
National Theatre Live showing of "Amadeus" at 7 p.m. at McMillan Memorial Library. This event is free.
Silver Foxes present "Nagging, American Style" at 1:30 p.m. at the Retirement Community of Nekoosa, 145 N. Cedar St. in Nekoosa. This event is free.
British comic, Sajeela Kershi performs "Shallow Halal" at 7 p.m. at the WRCT auditorium. This event is $15 and recommended for adult audiences.
Theater games with audience participation, "Atlas Improv!" starts at 8:30 p.m. at the WRCT auditorium. This event is $15.
An evening including both shows and refreshments is available for $25.
Kat Cavanaugh leads a book discussion on "Eligible" by Curtis Sittenfeld, a modern version of "Pride and Prejudice" at 9 a.m. at the Alexander House. This event is free.
Film showing of "Mansfield Park," at 11 a.m. at McMillan Memorial Library. This event is free.
A discussion of Jane Austen's early writings at 1 p.m. at the South Wood County Historical Museum, 540 Third St. S. in Wisconsin Rapids. This event is free.
Central Wisconsin Area and Wisconsin Rapids community theatres present a staged reading of "Emma" at 3 p.m. at WRCT auditorium. This event is free.
Champagne Piano Concert at 6:30 p.m. at the Central Wisconsin Cultural Center. This event is $20.
For more information, or for a full list of events and details, visit wrctheatre.org/artigras/events.asp.
